steps.title: Connect claim to evidence
steps.content: The passage first says that some of the 206 ticker-tape parades honored aviation achievements. The next sentence gives a specific confirming detail: Bennett's parade was one of sixteen parades honoring pilots.

steps.title: Rule out wrong relationships
steps.content: A would merely add a separate point, but Bennett's parade is evidence for the previous claim. B would show a result or conclusion, though the count does not follow from the prior sentence. D would contrast with the previous sentence. The transition should reinforce the aviation-parade claim. The answer is C.
